data.title: Nebius Weighs AI21 Labs Deal As CoreWeave Capacity Tests Growth Story
data.description:
• Nebius Group (NasdaqGS:NBIS) is in active talks to acquire Israeli AI firm AI21 Labs after Nvidia exited earlier negotiations.
  • The company is also expanding its AI infrastructure footprint through new data center capacity contracts with cloud specialist CoreWeave.
  • These developments come as Nebius shares trade at $144.96, following a 61.2% return year to date and a very large 1 year gain.

Nebius Group, ticker NasdaqGS:NBIS, is pursuing AI21 Labs at a time when its own share price sits at $144.96 and recent returns have been strong. The stock is up 61.2% year to date and around 7x over the past 1 year, which puts extra attention on how new AI assets and infrastructure deals might shape the company’s next phase.

For investors, the AI21 Labs talks and new CoreWeave contracts highlight how Nebius is positioning its AI software stack alongside capacity in key data center markets. The outcome of the acquisition negotiations, together with execution on these infrastructure agreements, may influence the company’s future product mix, customer reach, and how the durability of recent share price gains is perceived.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Nebius Group by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Nebius Group.

2 things going right for Nebius Group that this headline doesn't cover.

This potential acquisition of AI21 Labs sits alongside growing infrastructure commitments and helps fill out Nebius Group’s AI stack. AI21 Labs focuses on large language models and enterprise AI tools, which could give Nebius more of its own higher level software to run on the GPU capacity it is already lining up with partners such as CoreWeave and TD SYNNEX. That matters if Nebius wants to capture not just spending on raw compute, but also spending on applied AI use cases where customers compare it with Amazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud. At the same time, more data center build outs and a sizeable software acquisition would add to capital needs and execution complexity, which investors are already watching given analyst comments on funding, earnings quality, and valuation checks. The key question is whether combining AI21 Labs, new CoreWeave contracts, and existing deployments like TD SYNNEX’s reserved NVIDIA clusters on Nebius AI Cloud can support durable usage and revenue without stretching the balance sheet or increasing operational risk too far.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Nebius Group Narrative

  • The AI21 Labs talks and new CoreWeave data center capacity support the existing narrative that Nebius is building full stack AI infrastructure backed by large partners and long term contracts.
  • Adding another AI asset and more contracted capacity could amplify earlier concerns in the narrative about high capital requirements and the need to secure power, funding, and regulatory approvals across multiple regions.
  • The possibility that Nebius could pair in house foundation models from AI21 Labs with its own Aether cloud upgrade and TD SYNNEX distribution appears to be only partly reflected in the narrative, which focuses more on large hyperscale contracts.

data.description:

For investors, this development sits alongside the core story of Estée Lauder Companies as a global beauty group, with brands across skin care, makeup, fragrance and hair care. Large consumer names continue to face closer scrutiny on how they handle customer data, especially when business models rely on loyalty programs, direct to consumer channels and online sales.

If the settlement is approved, it may help bring more clarity around the financial and legal impact of the 2023 breaches for NYSE:EL. The broader consideration for investors is how the company’s data security practices and disclosures change over time, and what that might mean for customer confidence and long term brand trust.

The proposed CAD 1,515,000 settlement helps frame the financial impact of the 2023 Canadian data breaches for Estée Lauder Companies, at least for this specific group of class members. For you as an investor, the amount looks manageable relative to the size of a global beauty group, but it still reflects a cost tied to information security and legal processes rather than operations or growth. The bigger issue is often not the one off cash outlay but what it signals about data governance, internal controls and the potential for similar incidents in other regions or channels. Large consumer groups such as L'Oréal, Coty and Shiseido are all under pressure to protect customer data as loyalty programs and e commerce become more important, so investors increasingly treat cybersecurity and privacy as part of operational quality.   • The existing narrative around growth, restructuring and digital expansion does not explicitly account for potential future data security incidents or regulatory actions, which could influence both risk assessments and required returns.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Wayfair (W) After Its Sharp Share Price Swings?
data.description:
• This article examines whether Wayfair, at around US$75.77, may still be offering value or if most of the potential upside is already reflected in the price, and breaks down what the current price might be implying.
  • The stock has experienced notable volatility, with a 6.6% decline over the past week, a 6.0% gain over the last 30 days, a 28.9% decline year to date, and a 144.3% return over the past year.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Wayfair's position in online home retail and how investors are reassessing growth potential and risk in that area. This context helps explain why the share price has moved significantly over periods as short as a week and as long as several years.
  • Wayfair currently has a valuation score of 3 out of 6. The rest of this article will explore what different valuation approaches indicate about that score and conclude with a broader perspective on what the market may be pricing in.

Wayfair delivered 144.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Specialty Retail industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Wayfair Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return.

For Wayfair, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $263 million, and analysts plus extrapolated estimates point to free cash flow of around $1.33 billion by 2030. Intermediate projections between 2026 and 2035 are used to build a path from current cash generation to that longer term figure, with Simply Wall St extending the estimates beyond the analyst horizon.

When all of those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about US$168.26 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around US$75.77, the DCF output indicates the stock is about 55.0% below this estimated value on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Wayfair is undervalued by 55.0%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Wayfair.

Approach 2: Wayfair Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are limited or volatile, the P/S ratio is often more useful than P/E or P/B because it focuses on revenue rather than profits or book value. It gives a clearer view of how much investors are paying for each dollar of sales.

Growth expectations and risk still matter. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can be associated with a higher “normal” P/S, while slower growth or higher risk generally align with a lower multiple.

Wayfair currently trades at a P/S of about 0.80x. This is above the Specialty Retail industry average of around 0.45x, but below the peer group average of roughly 1.27x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary Fair Ratio for Wayfair is 0.68x, which estimates what the P/S might be based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and risks. Because it blends these company-specific inputs, the Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the industry alone.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 0.68x with the actual P/S of 0.80x indicates that the shares are pricing in somewhat higher expectations than this model implies.

Result: OVERVALUED
